questions.text: Where does gas exchange take place in human lungs?
questions.explanation: Alveoli are tiny sacs where oxygen and carbon dioxide are exchanged.
The diaphragm moves downwards to expand the chest cavity during inhalation.

questions.text: Oxygen is transported in blood mainly by:
questions.explanation: Hemoglobin in red blood cells binds oxygen for transport.

questions.text: External respiration refers to gas exchange between:
questions.explanation: External respiration occurs at the alveoli between air and blood.

questions.text: The respiratory rhythm centre is located in the:
questions.explanation: The medulla oblongata controls breathing rate and depth.

questions.text: The respiratory quotient is the ratio of:
questions.explanation: RQ = volume of CO2 released / volume of O2 used.
